term_inventory_hebrew.meaning:
1) internal organs, inward parts, bowels, intestines, belly
1a) inward parts
1b) digestive organs
1c) organs of procreation, womb
1d) place of emotions or distress or love (fig.)
1e) external belly

1) breath, spirit
1a) breath (of God)
1b) breath (of man)
1c) every breathing thing
1d) spirit (of man)
Aramaic equivalent: nish.ma (H5396) 'breath'

term_inventory_hebrew.step_search_flag: STEP presents as 9 sub-glosses: soul, soul: animal, soul: appetite, soul: dead, soul: life, soul: myself, soul: neck, soul: person — all resolving to H5315. STEP internal suffixed identifiers: H5315G, H5315H, H5315I, H5315J, H5315K, H5315L, H5315M, H5315N. Consolidated as single entry per researcher decision.
term_inventory_hebrew.meaning:
1) soul, self, life, creature, person, appetite, mind, living being, desire, emotion, passion
1a) that which breathes, the breathing substance or being, soul, the inner being of man
1b) living being
1c) living being (with life in the blood)
1d) the man himself, self, person or individual
1e) seat of the appetites
1f) seat of emotions and passions
1g) activity of mind (1g1: uncertain)
1h) activity of the will (1h1: uncertain)
1i) activity of the character (1i1: uncertain)

STEP sub-gloss facets (same lexeme, H5315):
  G-suffix: soul (core inner being)
  H-suffix: soul: life (ne.phesh as animating life principle)
  I-suffix: soul: myself (ne.phesh as reflexive self/pronoun)
  J-suffix: soul: person (ne.phesh as countable individual)
  K-suffix: soul: animal (ne.phesh applied to non-human living creatures)
  L-suffix: soul: appetite (ne.phesh as seat of physical desire/craving)
  M-suffix: soul: dead (ne.phesh of a corpse — dead soul/dead body)
  N-suffix: soul: neck (ne.phesh via throat/neck, the breath-organ)

term_inventory_greek.step_search_flag: STEP presents as 5 sub-glosses: soul, soul: animal, soul: life, soul: myself, soul: person — all resolving to G5590. STEP internal suffixed identifiers: G5590G, G5590H, G5590I, G5590J, G5590K. Consolidated as single entry per researcher decision.
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: psuchē: life; departed spirit, ghost; the immaterial and immortal soul (first in Pindar); the conscious self or personality as centre of emotions, desires, and affections; of various aspects of the self (enduring heart, poor-spirited); of the emotional self; of the moral and intellectual self; of animals; of inanimate things (the soul of a city/constitution); Philosophical uses: in early physicists, the primary substance, source of life and consciousness; the spirit of the universe; in Plato, the immaterial principle of movement and life, with tripartite division into logismos, thumos, and epithumia; in Neo-Platonists, characterised by discursive thinking, related to nous as image to archetype; also: butterfly or moth; Psyche in the allegory of Psyche and Eros. Etymological note: the Homeric psuchē is rather warm blood than breath, escaping through a wound.

And the LORD sent an angel, who cut off all the mighty warriors and commanders and officers in the camp of the king of Assyria. So he returned with shame of face to his own land. And when he came into the house of his god, some of his own sons struck him down there with the sword.

His arms are rods of gold, set with jewels. His body is polished ivory, bedecked with sapphires.

Listen to me, O coastlands, and give attention, you peoples from afar. The LORD called me from the womb, from the body of my mother he named my name.
Look down from heaven and see, from your holy and beautiful habitation. Where are your zeal and your might? The stirring of your inner parts and your compassion are held back from me.
My anguish, my anguish! I writhe in pain! Oh the walls of my heart! My heart is beating wildly; I cannot keep silent, for I hear the sound of the trumpet, the alarm of war.

And Jonathan made David swear again by his love for him, for he loved him as he loved his own soul.

And the king swore, saying, "As the LORD lives, who has redeemed my soul out of every adversity,"

that the LORD may establish his word that he spoke concerning me, saying, 'If your sons pay close attention to their way, to walk before me in faithfulness with all their heart and with all their soul, you shall not lack a man on the throne of Israel.'

if they repent with all their heart and with all their soul in the land of their enemies, who carried them captive, and pray to you toward their land, which you gave to their fathers, the city that you have chosen, and the house that I have built for your name,

lest like a lion they tear my soul apart, rending it in pieces, with none to deliver.

For you will not abandon my soul to Sheol, or let your holy one see corruption.

He restores my soul. He leads me in paths of righteousness for his name's sake.
To you, O LORD, I lift up my soul.
O LORD, you have brought up my soul from Sheol; you restored me to life from among those who go down to the pit.
Be gracious to me, O LORD, for I am in distress; my eye is wasted from grief; my soul and my body also.
My soul makes its boast in the LORD; let the humble hear and be glad.
Then my soul will rejoice in the LORD, exulting in his salvation.
As a deer pants for flowing streams, so pants my soul for you, O God.
Why are you cast down, O my soul, and why are you in turmoil within me? Hope in God; for I shall again praise him, my salvation
and my God. My soul is cast down within me; therefore I remember you from the land of Jordan and of Hermon, from Mount Mizar.
O God, you are my God; earnestly I seek you; my soul thirsts for you; my flesh faints for you, as in a dry and weary land where there is no water.
My soul will be satisfied as with fat and rich food, and my mouth will praise you with joyful lips,
My soul clings to you; your right hand upholds me.
Save me, O God! For the waters have come up to my neck.
My soul longs, yes, faints for the courts of the LORD; my heart and flesh sing for joy to the living God.
Gladden the soul of your servant, for to you, O Lord, do I lift up my soul.
Bless the LORD, O my soul, and all that is within me, bless his holy name!
hungry and thirsty, their soul fainted within them.
For he satisfies the longing soul, and the hungry soul he fills with good things.
My soul is consumed with longing for your rules at all times.
My soul longs for your salvation; I hope in your word.
But I have calmed and quieted my soul, like a weaned child with its mother; like a weaned child is my soul within me.
I stretch out my hands to you; my soul thirsts for you like a parched land. Selah
for wisdom will come into your heart, and knowledge will be pleasant to your soul;
The soul of the sluggard craves and gets nothing, while the soul of the diligent is richly supplied.
A desire fulfilled is sweet to the soul, but to turn away from evil is an abomination to fools.
Gracious words are like a honeycomb, sweetness to the soul and health to the body.
The soul of the wicked desires evil; his neighbor finds no mercy in his eyes.
Like cold water to a thirsty soul, so is good news from a far country.

If a man fathers a hundred children and lives many years, so that the days of his years are many, but his soul is not satisfied with life's good things, and he also has no burial, I say that a stillborn child is better off than he.
Tell me, you whom my soul loves, where you pasture your flock, where you make it lie down at noon;
On my bed by night I sought him whom my soul loves; I sought him, but found him not.
I opened to my beloved, but my beloved had turned and gone. My soul failed me when he spoke. I sought him, but found him not; I called him, but he gave no answer.
Your new moons and your appointed feasts my soul hates; they have become a burden to me; I am weary of bearing them.
My soul yearns for you in the night; my spirit within me earnestly seeks you. For when your judgments are in the earth, the inhabitants of the world learn righteousness.
Behold my servant, whom I uphold, my chosen, in whom my soul delights; I have put my Spirit upon him; he will bring forth justice to the nations.

cross_registry_links.note: H5315 ne.phesh was provisionally owned by desire (043) before ownership was transferred to soul (182). Registry 043 retains also_used_in reference.
Isa 53:11 ('out of the anguish of his soul') links ne.phesh directly to the anguish word set.
Multiple ne.phesh verses express soul grief (Psa 31:9; Job 30:25; Eze 27:31 'bitterness of soul'). Overlap with grief registry expected.
Lam 3:24 ('The LORD is my portion, says my soul, therefore I will hope in him') links ne.phesh to the hope word set. Psa 130:5 similarly.
Lam 3:17 ('my soul is bereft of peace') and Jer 6:16 ('find rest for your souls') link ne.phesh to the peace word set.
Psuchē as the locus of fear and trembling in several NT passages; also G5590 related word oligopsuchos (fainthearted) is directly relevant to the fear registry.

data_quality_flags.description: STEP presents H5315 ne.phesh under 8 sub-gloss entries using suffixed identifiers (H5315G, H5315H, H5315I, H5315J, H5315K, H5315L, H5315M, H5315N). These are STEP internal sub-gloss markers, not distinct Strong's numbers. Source-file authoritative number is H5315. MTI record retains H5315. Researcher approved consolidation into single term entry with all sub-gloss facets documented in the meaning field.

STEP presents G5590 psuchē under 5 sub-gloss entries using suffixed identifiers (G5590G, G5590H, G5590I, G5590J, G5590K). These are STEP internal sub-gloss markers, not distinct Strong's numbers. Source-file authoritative number is G5590. MTI record retains G5590. Researcher approved consolidation into single term entry with all sub-gloss facets documented in the meaning field.

H5315 ne.phesh has an occurrence count of approximately 753. The source file contains 683 verses in the shared verse block. This JSON captures a representative subset of approximately 150 verses (approximately 20% of the 683 source-file verses). The full 683-verse set is available in word_soul.md lines 1216–2527. Researcher is aware that the full verse set has not been individually enumerated in this JSON due to the exceptional size of the term.

Not a data gap: ne.phesh is the richest term in the dataset (753 occurrences). Flag applied only to note that verse_records for this term are a representative sample rather than exhaustive enumeration. Full extraction deferred to database build phase.

H4578 me.eh (belly/bowels) appears in the soul registry as a somatic anchor term — it denotes the physical location of inner-being states (compassion, yearning, anguish). Notably, Jer 31:20 uses me.eh for God's yearning for Ephraim, and Isa 63:15 for the stirring of God's inner parts. This term bridges the somatic and inner-being domains and may prove analytically significant in Session B.
